places.name: Restaurant 1910 · Gourmet by Hausers (Hotel Belvedere)
places.near: In the village on Dorfstrasse, walkable from Grindelwald Terminal/train station
places.order: The multi-course tasting menu with wine pairing; technically refined, reduced-yet-flavourful compositions by chef Dávid Imre Rózsa
places.notes: One MICHELIN star; the hotel's main Restaurant Belvedere is a separate, more casual Swiss/international dining room
places.famousFor: 1 MICHELIN star (2025) and 16 Gault&Millau points; intimate 6-table gourmet room inside the 4-star superior Hotel Belvedere
places.goodToKnow: Reservations essential; evenings only, limited seating; smart dress. Best for a couples' splurge dinner

places.name: Restaurant Fiescherblick
places.cuisine: Creative regional / French with Japanese & Scandinavian influences
places.near: Village centre next to the church, short walk from the station
places.order: The seasonal 'Chef's Choice' menu (4, 5 or 6 courses) with optional wine pairing, by chef Aurélien Mettler
places.notes: One MICHELIN star
places.famousFor: 1 MICHELIN star and 16 Gault&Millau points (2025); one of the highest-rated tables in the village
places.goodToKnow: Open Tuesday to Saturday; reservations essential; views toward the Eiger north face, Fiescherhorn and Männlichen

places.name: GLACIER Fine Dining (Boutique Hotel Glacier)
places.near: In the chic Boutique Hotel Glacier, a short walk/drive from the village centre (Endweg)
places.order: The 5- or 7-course tasting menu with the recommended wine pairing; à la carte also available
places.famousFor: 16 Gault&Millau points; floor-to-ceiling windows and a terrace with an Eiger view; wine cellar of ~500 labels (200+ by the glass)
places.goodToKnow: One of the most romantic/Instagrammable dining rooms in Grindelwald; book the terrace for sunset; reservations recommended

places.name: Restaurant Schmitte (Romantik Hotel Schweizerhof)
places.cuisine: Swiss / Alpine / international à la carte
places.near: Village centre, steps from Grindelwald Terminal/train station
places.order: Seasonal Alpine-inspired plates and Swiss classics; strong wine list
places.famousFor: Elegant à la carte dining in the 5-star Romantik Hotel Schweizerhof; listed in the MICHELIN Guide
places.goodToKnow: Romantic setting; the hotel also has an Eiger-view spa terrace; reservations advised

places.name: Barry's Restaurant, Bar & Lounge (Eiger Mountain & Soul Resort / Hotel Eiger)
places.near: Village centre on Dorfstrasse, a few minutes' walk from Grindelwald Terminal/train station
places.order: Raclette, cheese fondue and other Swiss classics; seasonal international mains
places.famousFor: One of the most popular tables in the village (4.3 stars, 1,700+ reviews); cosy romantic niches and reliable Swiss classics
places.goodToKnow: Wide-appeal menu good for families and couples alike; busy in season, so reserve. Has a bar/lounge but the restaurant itself is dining-focused

places.name: Restaurant Pinte (Hotel Spinne)
places.near: On Dorfstrasse in the village centre, easy walk from the station
places.order: Swiss classics with seasonal ingredients; fondue and hearty mains
places.famousFor: Traditional Swiss cooking right on the main street; a convenient, well-regarded après-hike dinner spot
places.goodToKnow: Central and family-friendly; part of Hotel Spinne (which also runs an Italian restaurant, Piazza)

places.name: Cafe 3692 (Kitchen-Republic)
places.near: On Terrassenweg on the sunny terrace above the village; walkable or a short drive/bus from the centre
places.order: Homemade cakes and brunch plates by day; creative seasonal dishes; excellent coffee
places.notes: Opening days vary seasonally (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: A design-forward, much-loved café-restaurant on the scenic Terrassenweg with mountain views; ~4.8-star reputation
places.goodToKnow: Limited opening days (typically Fri–Sun) and evening hours; check current schedule before going. Very Instagrammable

places.name: Onkel Tom's Hütte (Onkel Tom's Pizza & Wine)
places.cuisine: Wood-fired pizza (also rösti/fondue) + large wine list
places.near: On Dorfstrasse in the village, walkable from the centre
places.order: Wood-oven pizza; pair with a bottle from the extensive list; rösti and fondue also available
places.famousFor: Tiny, atmospheric wooden ski-hut room with 400+ wines and beloved pizzas; a Grindelwald institution
places.goodToKnow: Very small and popular — reservations strongly recommended; cosy for couples but works for families too

places.name: Ristorante Pizzeria Da Salvi (Hotel Steinbock)
places.near: Central, ~5 min from the station and right by the First cableway base station
places.order: Wood-fired pizza (the Carbonara pizza is a favourite); pasta, fish, and grilled meats
places.famousFor: The go-to family pizzeria in Grindelwald; authentic wood-oven pizza and a broad Italian menu (620+ reviews)
places.goodToKnow: Family-friendly, generous portions, open all day; good for a relaxed dinner with kids

places.name: Restaurant Bodmi / BellaVista (Hotel Bodmi)
places.area: Terrassenweg / Bodmi Arena, above the village
places.near: On Terrassenweg by the Bodmi Arena, a short drive/bus or walk up from the village
places.order: Seasonal Swiss mains and well-presented international dishes; terrace lunch with a view
places.famousFor: Elegant Swiss/international dining with a panoramic terrace overlooking the Bodmi nursery-slope area and mountains
places.goodToKnow: Great sunny terrace; family-friendly given the adjacent kids' snow playground; romantic at sunset

places.name: Challi-Stübli (Hotel Kreuz & Post)
places.near: Central on Dorfstrasse, walkable from the station
places.order: Cheese fondue, raclette, rösti and other Swiss classics
places.famousFor: Atmospheric room built from an original 1748 Simmental barn; classic Swiss cheese and meat specialities
places.goodToKnow: Cosy, traditional ambience ideal for a fondue night; the hotel also has an à la carte restaurant

places.name: BG's Grill (Bergwelt Grindelwald – Alpine Design Resort)
places.cuisine: Grill — premium meat & fish, modern seasonal
places.near: At the Bergwelt resort in the village (Bergwelt 4), short walk/drive from the centre
places.order: First-class grilled meat and fish; the 6- or 7-course 'Chef's Choice' tasting menu
places.famousFor: Chef Urs Gschwend's grill in a stylish alpine design resort; ambitious modern 'Chef's Choice' menu plus a terrace with mountain panorama
places.goodToKnow: Sleek, design-led setting good for couples; reserve ahead. The resort has multiple dining venues

places.name: Restaurant Aspen (Aspen Alpin Lifestyle Hotel)
places.near: Up the hillside in the Aspen area above the village; best reached by car or hotel access (uphill from the centre)
places.order: Swiss classics and seasonal mains; the terrace at sunset
places.famousFor: Elevated terrace dining with sweeping mountain views over the valley
places.goodToKnow: Panoramic, peaceful setting; romantic for couples and comfortable for families

places.name: La Marmite (Hotel Derby) / Derby Föhrenstube
places.near: In Hotel Derby right by Grindelwald Terminal/train station, very central
places.order: Cheese fondue and Swiss beef stew; regional seasonal plates
places.notes: Restaurant naming (La Marmite vs Föhrenstube) may vary by season (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: Traditional Swiss dishes (cheese fondue, beef stew) with fresh, locally sourced ingredients; friendly service
places.goodToKnow: Convenient location for arrivals; the Föhrenstube is the cosy Swiss room. Good family option

places.name: Restaurant Eiger Stübli (Hotel Eigerblick / Eiger area)
places.near: In the village, walkable from the centre
places.order: Berner Platte (Bernese meat platter), fondue and other Swiss classics
places.notes: Exact street address not confirmed online (Needs verification) (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: Authentic local charm and Swiss favourites, including the classic Berner Platte
places.goodToKnow: Cosy, traditional atmosphere; good for a hearty Swiss dinner

places.name: Avocado Bar
places.near: Village centre on Dorfstrasse, easy walk from the station
places.order: Grain/veg bowls, big salads, smoothies and breakfast plates
places.famousFor: Fresh, nutritious bowls and salads in the village centre; popular, colourful, Instagram-friendly plates (~4.6 stars)
places.goodToKnow: Despite the name it's a daytime café-style eatery, not an alcohol-focused bar; good lighter/vegetarian option for families and couples

places.name: Ringgenberg Bäckerei & Tea Room
places.cuisine: Swiss bakery, cakes, pastries, light lunch
places.near: On Dorfstrasse in the village centre, walkable from the station
places.order: Fresh pastries, cakes and tarts; coffee and a light lunch
places.famousFor: Long-standing village bakery-café known for fresh breads, cakes and tarts
places.goodToKnow: Great family/kid-friendly breakfast and coffee-and-cake stop; not a dinner venue

places.name: Golden India
places.near: In the village, walkable from the centre
places.order: Curries, tandoori dishes and fresh naan
places.notes: Exact address to confirm (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: Grindelwald's go-to Indian kitchen — authentic curries, tandoori and naan; a popular change of pace from Swiss fare
places.goodToKnow: Good vegetarian selection; family-friendly. Verify exact address/hours locally

places.name: Restaurant Piazza (Hotel Spinne)
places.near: On Dorfstrasse in the village centre, walkable from the station
places.order: Pasta and risotto dishes; Italian mains
places.famousFor: Authentic Italian pasta and risotto in the village centre, part of Hotel Spinne
places.goodToKnow: Central, family-friendly Italian alternative to Da Salvi

places.name: Stallbeizli-Heuboden
places.near: Outside the main street, up toward the hillside/pastures — best reached by car; check access and opening
places.order: Traditional Swiss classics; hearty regional plates
places.notes: Hidden gem; verify location, access and opening hours (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: A characterful chalet-style 'stall' restaurant in the mountains serving classic Swiss food; very high ratings (small, hidden-gem feel)
places.goodToKnow: A bit off the beaten path from the centre; limited seating and seasonal hours — reserve and confirm before going
places.address: Stallbeizli Heuboden, 2a, Itramenstrasse, Auf der Gey, Grindelwald, Verwaltungskreis Interlaken-Oberhasli, Verwaltungsregion Oberland, Bern/Berne, 3818, Schweiz/Suisse/Svizzera/Svizra

places.name: Berggasthaus First
places.near: Take the Firstbahn gondola from Grindelwald to First; the restaurant is at the top station
places.order: Rösti, cheese fondue, bratwurst and house-baked sweets from local ingredients
places.famousFor: Best-rated restaurant right beside the First gondola top station with panoramic Eiger/Wetterhorn views; large sun terrace; also an overnight mountain lodge
places.goodToKnow: Self-service, serviced restaurant and panoramic terrace (up to ~300 seats); superb sunset/photo spot at the First cliff-walk. Depends on lift operating hours

places.name: Bergrestaurant Schreckfeld
places.near: On the Firstbahn gondola line — get off at Schreckfeld station (50 m from the restaurant)
places.order: Schreckfeldrösti; home-style Swiss mains using regional products
places.famousFor: Friendly mid-station restaurant on the First gondola line with a big terrace (~250 seats) and mountain views; known for its Schreckfeld-Rösti
places.goodToKnow: Family-friendly; broad menu for all ages; tied to gondola operating hours

places.name: Bort Schyr / Restaurant Alpinhotel Bort
places.near: Take the Firstbahn gondola and get off at Bort mid-station
places.order: Fondue, Älplermagronen and rösti; Swiss and international classics
places.notes: Bort Schyr and the Alpinhotel Bort restaurant are adjacent Bort venues (Needs verification of which is open in your season)
places.famousFor: Cosy wood-beamed alpine restaurant at the Bort gondola mid-station with big windows and valley views; part of the Alpinhotel Bort
places.setting: Mountain restaurant / alpine hotel (reachable by lift)
places.goodToKnow: Warm, homely alpine feel; good family stop on the way up/down First; can also stay overnight. Depends on lift hours

places.name: Bergrestaurant Bussalp
places.near: Reach Bussalp by the Grindelwald–Bussalp postbus/mountain bus (or on foot); a well-known winter sledding start point
places.order: Hearty soups, classic cheese dishes and mountain-style mains from seasonal, local ingredients
places.famousFor: Newly rebuilt (autumn 2025) mountain restaurant deep in the Grindelwald alps with a big sun terrace, AlpLounge sofas and Kneipp bath; family hiking hub
places.goodToKnow: Great for families and groups; sun-terrace views; new building. Access depends on bus/season

places.name: Bergrestaurant Pfingstegg
places.near: 10-minute ride on the Pfingsteggbahn cable car from Grindelwald to Pfingstegg
places.order: Regional cheese specialities; Swiss classics on the terrace
places.famousFor: Rustic mountain restaurant with a large terrace and magnificent views over Grindelwald toward the Eiger and Wetterhorn; sits between the two glaciers
places.goodToKnow: Family favourite (toboggan run and Fly-Line nearby); seasonal — typically opens around May. Depends on cable-car hours

places.name: Berghotel Grosse Scheidegg
places.area: Grosse Scheidegg pass (~1,962 m), above Grindelwald
places.near: Grindelwald–Grosse Scheidegg postbus (road closed to private cars); also reachable on foot
places.order: House specialities and Swiss classics on the panorama terrace or in the cosy stube
places.famousFor: Historic pass-top berghotel between Grindelwald and Meiringen with a panoramic terrace; classic starting point for hikes and bike tours
places.goodToKnow: Remote alpine setting with big views; seasonal opening tied to the postbus. Good scenic lunch stop for families and couples

places.name: Bargelegg Hütte
places.near: In the First hiking area above Grindelwald — reached on foot from First/Bort; check access route and hours
places.order: Cheese fondue and raclette; hearty hut classics
places.notes: Hidden gem; verify exact location and opening (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: Cosy alpine hut serving fondue and raclette from fresh, locally sourced ingredients, with a large sun terrace in a peaceful setting
places.goodToKnow: Genuine hidden-gem hut atmosphere; seasonal/limited hours, best confirmed before hiking out
places.address: Bärgelegghütte, 1a, Bärgelegg, Uf Luegen, Grindelwald, Verwaltungskreis Interlaken-Oberhasli, Verwaltungsregion Oberland, Bern/Berne, 3818, Schweiz/Suisse/Svizzera/Svizra

places.name: Fly-Line Pfingstegg
places.near: Take the Pfingstegg aerial cableway from Grindelwald; the Fly-Line starts just below the mountain station
places.order: A 2.5-minute, 350m harnessed glide at 8-12 km/h through the forest up to ~12m off the ground, with a retriever pulling you back up
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: The Fly-Line at Pfingstegg above Grindelwald, billed as the only installation in the world where you don't have to walk back to the start
places.goodToKnow: Gentle and family-friendly (not allowed under age 4); closes in wet or stormy weather, and pairs well with the Pfingstegg toboggan run

places.name: Pfingstegg - Grindelwald Paradise
places.near: Pfingstegg aerial cableway from the edge of Grindelwald village in the Jungfrau region
places.order: The 736m rail toboggan run, the Fly-Line forest glide, and the panoramic terrace and hikes toward the Bäregg and glacier gorges
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Pfingstegg above Grindelwald, a sun terrace and adventure spot with the toboggan run and Fly-Line facing the Eiger glaciers
places.goodToKnow: A good half-day family base; activities are weather-dependent and the cableway runs roughly April-October

places.name: Rodelbahn Pfingstegg
places.near: Pfingstegg cable car on the south edge of Grindelwald, ~15 min walk from the station or bus stop 'Pfingsteggbahn'
places.order: The self-paced channel toboggan run after riding the Pfingstegg cable car up to 1,346 m
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Pfingstegg summer toboggan run above Grindelwald, the only one in the Jungfrau region, with views across the valley to the glaciers; also has a Fly-Line
places.goodToKnow: Reached only by cable car or a 1-hour hike; age 8+ may ride alone. Closes in wet weather; tickets on site only.

places.name: Firstbahn AG
places.near: First cableway base station in Grindelwald village, Bernese Oberland; ~25 min by gondola to First (2,168 m).
places.order: The First Cliff Walk by Tissot (45 m platform over the cliff), the First Flyer zipline (up to 84 km/h), and mountain carts/Trotti bikes.
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Firstbahn / Grindelwald First - the aerial cableway and 'Top of Adventure' summit above Grindelwald, home to the First Cliff Walk and First Flyer zipline.
places.goodToKnow: Combine gondola plus 1-2 adventure activities on an Adventure Package; Flyer/Glider have a minimum age of 12. Weather-dependent.

places.name: Boutique Hotel Glacier
places.near: Bern/Zurich airports ~2-3hr by train; in Grindelwald village, gateway to Jungfrau region
places.order: Best for Eiger-facing rooms, the cozy alpine setting, and a base for hiking and skiing
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Family-run boutique hotel in Grindelwald with classic views of the Eiger and Bernese Alps
places.goodToKnow: $$$, intimate boutique hotel with mountain views in the Jungfrau ski region

places.name: Hotel Kreuz & Post
places.near: Dorfstrasse, central Grindelwald, steps from Grindelwald train station
places.order: Best for an Eiger-view stay with rooftop wellness in the Jungfrau region
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Central Grindelwald family hotel with rooftop spa and direct Eiger views
places.goodToKnow: 4-star, $$$; rooftop sauna/steam wellness, free WiFi

places.name: BERGWELT GRINDELWALD | ALPINE DESIGN RESORT
places.near: In Grindelwald village; train to Grindelwald, then short transfer
places.order: Best for design-led mountain stays; spa and panoramic dining
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Modern alpine design resort with Eiger views in Grindelwald
places.goodToKnow: 4-star superior, $$$-$$$$; spa, pool, Jungfrau region views

places.name: Aspen
places.near: Elevated above Grindelwald, Bernese Oberland; near Kleine Scheidegg/Mannlichen
places.order: Best for Eiger views; chalet-chic rooms, spa and award-winning restaurant
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: 4-star superior alpine-lifestyle hotel above Grindelwald facing the Eiger north face
places.goodToKnow: 4-star S; ~9 room types incl. Penthouse Suite; spa with outdoor whirlpool; $$$$

places.name: Grindelwald-First (Top of Adventure)
places.near: Six-seater gondola from the First station in Grindelwald village (Dorfstrasse), ~25 min ride with a mid-station at Bort.
places.order: Gondola return ticket (approx. CHF 76 May–Oct, CHF 72 off-season); pair with the Adventure Package (CHF ~69 for one activity up to ~CHF 99 for all four).
places.notes: Anchor experience of the day — allow a half to full day here.
places.famousFor: Grindelwald's flagship adventure summit with a cluster of thrill activities and jaw-dropping Eiger, Mönch and Wetterhorn panoramas — the region's most Instagrammed mountain playground.
places.goodToKnow: Adventure activities (Flyer, Glider, Cart, Trottibike) run roughly mid-May to end of October, weather permitting; gondola runs year-round. Free travel with Jungfrau Travel Pass, 50% off with Swiss Travel Pass / Half Fare Card / Berner Oberland Pass.

places.name: First Cliff Walk by Tissot
places.near: Walk directly from the First top station; the entrance is right beside the exit.
places.order: Included free with your Grindelwald-First gondola ticket — no separate fee. Go early morning or late afternoon for empty-railing photos.
places.notes: Signature couples/Instagram shot of Grindelwald.
places.famousFor: A metal walkway bolted to the sheer First cliff face, leading to a suspension footbridge and a viewing platform that juts 45 m out over the void — one of the most photogenic spots in the Alps.
places.goodToKnow: Typically accessible ~08:30–17:30 in season; can close in high wind, snow or ice. Free once you hold a gondola ticket.

places.name: First Flyer & First Glider Zip Lines
places.near: Board at the First top station (Flyer) / Schreckfeld; part of the Grindelwald-First activity network.
places.order: Book as single activities or as part of the 4-in-1 Adventure Package (excludes gondola). Flyer and Glider run between First and Schreckfeld.
places.notes: Adventure highlight; great for teens and thrill-seeking couples. (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: The First Flyer sends you screaming down a steel cable at up to ~84 km/h toward Schreckfeld; the First Glider hauls you back up face-down like a bird in flight over the alpine slopes.
places.goodToKnow: Summer-only, roughly mid-May–end of October; height/weight limits apply; closes in bad weather. Reserve on busy summer days.

places.name: First Mountain Cart & Trottibike Descent
places.near: Mountain Carts start at Schreckfeld (one gondola stop below First); Trottibikes continue from Bort down to the village.
places.order: Combine Mountain Cart + Trottibike as part of the Adventure Package; a single Trottibike ride is ~CHF 21/adult.
places.notes: Best done as the fun way to descend after the Cliff Walk and Bachalpsee. (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: Zig-zagging down the mountain in a low three-wheeled Mountain Cart (Schreckfeld–Bort), then continuing on an oversized Trottibike scooter (Bort–Grindelwald) — a family favourite combo.
places.goodToKnow: Carts/Trottis typically run ~10:00–15:30, roughly mid-May to end of October, weather dependent; minimum age/height limits apply.
places.address: Schreckfeld / Bort stations, Grindelwald-First, 3818 Grindelwald, Switzerland

places.name: Bachalpsee (Bachsee) Lake Hike
places.near: Take the First gondola, then follow the well-signed trail from the top station.
places.order: Easy ~3.1 km (one way) walk from First; ~1 hour each way on a wide, mostly stroller-manageable path. Go at first gondola (08:30) for glassy reflections and no crowds.
places.notes: Combine with the Cliff Walk for the classic First day. (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: A mirror-still alpine lake reflecting the Wetterhorn, Schreckhorn and Finsteraarhorn — arguably the single most photographed lake in the Bernese Oberland, reached on a gentle, family-friendly path.
places.goodToKnow: Snow-free and best roughly June–October; extremely busy mid-morning in July–August; can hold snow/ice into early summer.

places.name: Faulhorn Summit Hike
places.near: Continue on foot beyond Bachalpsee from Grindelwald-First; alternatively reach it from Schynige Platte on a long ridge traverse.
places.order: From Bachalpsee it's ~2.4 km further with ~345 m of climbing; strong walkers only. Allow a full day round trip from First.
places.notes: For fitter hikers seeking a quieter, lesser-crowded high point. (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: A demanding continuation beyond Bachalpsee to a 2,681 m peak crowned by one of the oldest mountain hotels in the Alps (Berghotel Faulhorn), with a sweeping 360° panorama over the Eiger–Mönch–Jungfrau and toward Interlaken's lakes.
places.goodToKnow: High-alpine, summer-only (roughly July–October); check the historic Berghotel Faulhorn's seasonal opening before relying on food/lodging.

places.name: Bort Alpine Playground
places.near: Grindelwald-First gondola, alight at the Bort mid-station.
places.order: Free to play; access with your Grindelwald-First gondola ticket and get off at Bort mid-station. Restaurant and snack kiosk on site.
places.notes: Top family stop with big-mountain views.
places.famousFor: A 700 m² natural adventure playground under the Eiger, centred on an 8.5 m tall climbable wooden cow, with a dammable mountain stream, marmot- and squirrel-themed play, rope nets, balancing elements and outdoor bowling.
places.goodToKnow: Best in summer; snow-covered in winter. A natural break point when Trottibiking down from First.

places.name: Eiger Express Tricable Gondola
places.near: Departs from Grindelwald Terminal (Grundstrasse 54), directly reachable by train and with large car parking.
places.order: Ride it as the fast leg of a Jungfraujoch round trip, or ride up to Eigergletscher just for the Eiger-face views and the Eiger Trail.
places.notes: The modern gateway to the high mountains. (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: A state-of-the-art tricable gondola gliding beneath the notorious Eiger North Face, reaching Eigergletscher in ~15 minutes and cutting the trip to the Top of Europe by ~47 minutes versus the old route.
places.goodToKnow: Out of service for maintenance 20–24 April 2026 and 9–20 November 2026; seat reservation required on the onward Jungfraujoch train May–Oct (children also from CHF 10).

places.name: Jungfraujoch – Top of Europe
places.near: Eiger Express from Grindelwald Terminal to Eigergletscher, then the Jungfrau Railway; ~45 minutes door-to-summit.
places.order: Full Jungfraujoch round-trip ticket (via Eiger Express up + Jungfrau Railway); allow at least half a day. Ticket includes Sphinx, Ice Palace and Alpine Sensation.
places.notes: The bucket-list icon; go on a clear-sky day. (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: Europe's highest railway station, set on a glacier saddle between the Mönch and Jungfrau, with the Sphinx observation terrace, the Ice Palace carved into the Aletsch Glacier, the Alpine Sensation walk, and views over the Aletsch — the longest glacier in the Alps (UNESCO).
places.goodToKnow: Seat reservation mandatory May–October; open 365 days but weather can flatten the view — check the summit webcam before committing. Dress warmly (Ice Palace is ~−3 °C year-round); altitude can affect some visitors.
places.address: Jungfraujoch station, 3801 Fieschertal / accessed via Grindelwald Terminal, Switzerland

places.name: Männlichen Cableway & Royal Walk
places.near: Grindelwald–Männlichen gondola (GGM) from Grindelwald Terminal, or the Royal Ride cableway from Wengen.
places.order: Gondola up from Grindelwald Terminal (~19 min), do the ~25-min Royal Walk, then walk the Panorama Trail to Kleine Scheidegg and train back down.
places.notes: Gateway to the famous Panorama Trail.
places.famousFor: A panoramic ridge between the Grindelwald and Lauterbrunnen valleys; the short 'Royal Walk' climbs to the summit crown for a 360° sweep of the Eiger, Mönch, Jungfrau and Tschuggen.
places.goodToKnow: Summer gondola/cableway season ~23 May–25 October 2026. Summer offer: up to 50% off public transport to Männlichen plus 10% off the Wengen cable car, 1 July–31 Aug 2026.
places.address: Männlichenbahn, Grindelwald Terminal, Grundstrasse 54, 3818 Grindelwald, Switzerland

places.name: Panorama Trail: Männlichen to Kleine Scheidegg
places.near: Ride the gondola to Männlichen, then follow the well-marked Panoramaweg to Kleine Scheidegg.
places.order: Buy the combined ticket (gondola up to Männlichen + Wengernalp train down from Kleine Scheidegg); walk ~1.5 hours, mostly downhill/flat.
places.notes: The signature 'postcard' walk of the Jungfrau region. (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: One of Switzerland's most celebrated easy hikes — a nearly level ~4.5 km balcony path with the Eiger, Mönch and Jungfrau filling the view the entire way.
places.goodToKnow: Snow-free roughly late May–October; suitable for families and confident strollers on the graded sections. Combine with the Männlichen gondola season window.

places.name: Lieselotte Themed Family Trail (Männlichen → Holenstein)
places.near: Gondola to Männlichen, then walk the marked Lieselotte-Weg down to Holenstein.
places.order: Free to walk with your Männlichen gondola ticket; ~600 m descent, about 1.5 hours to the Holenstein mid-station.
places.notes: Excellent for families with young kids. (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: A storybook downhill trail following the cartoon cow Lieselotte, with 13 interactive stations (refresh the chickens, play a real alphorn, collect letters for a solution word) beneath the Eiger North Face.
places.goodToKnow: Not stroller-suitable; easiest walked June–October. Combine with the Sennenspielplatz playground (with the 8.5 m wooden Lieselotte) at the top.

places.name: Holenstein Treetop Path & Playground
places.near: Grindelwald–Männlichen gondola, alight at Holenstein.
places.order: Free to explore; reach it via the Männlichen gondola (Holenstein mid-station) or as the end of the Lieselotte Trail.
places.notes: Lesser-known but delightful stop for kids. (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: An elevated wooden 'treetop' walkway winding through the forest with netted suspension bridges, curvy slides, a zip line, viewing platforms and Lieselotte's house — a hidden family gem.
places.goodToKnow: Summer-only; tied to the Männlichen gondola operating season. Picnic area on site.

places.name: Pfingstegg Cable Car
places.near: Pfingstegg valley station is a short walk/bus from Grindelwald village centre.
places.order: Round-trip cable car; add the FunPackage combining the toboggan run and Fly-Line. Free travel with Berner Oberland Pass.
places.notes: Underrated, less-crowded alternative to First.
places.famousFor: A quick, short cable car to a sunny terrace facing the Eiger, Mättenberg and the twin Grindelwald glaciers — the base for the toboggan run, Fly-Line and quieter high-trails.
places.goodToKnow: 2026 summer season starts 25 April; mountain restaurant opens 9 May. Facility can close at short notice in poor weather.

places.name: Pfingstegg Summer Toboggan Run (Rodelbahn)
places.near: Take the Pfingstegg cable car up; the toboggan run is right at the top station.
places.order: Buy rides on site (single or multi-ride); no advance reservation possible. Bundle with the Fly-Line in the FunPackage.
places.notes: Great fun for families and couples alike.
places.famousFor: A 736 m rail-guided summer toboggan track dropping 58 m with speeds up to 40 km/h, banking through curves with the glaciers as a backdrop — you control your own brake.
places.goodToKnow: Cable car opens 25 April 2026 but the toboggan run typically opens later (around 22 May 2026) depending on snow/weather; closed in rain or wet conditions for safety.

places.name: Pfingstegg Fly-Line
places.near: At the Pfingstegg top station, reached by the cable car.
places.order: Buy on site; combine with the toboggan run via the FunPackage ticket.
places.notes: Pairs naturally with the Pfingstegg toboggan.
places.famousFor: A rail-suspended gliding line that swoops you along the mountainside above Grindelwald — a gentler, scenic flying experience the whole family can ride.
places.goodToKnow: Operates from the season start on 25 April 2026 (alongside the cable car); weather-dependent.

places.name: Grindelwald Glacier Gorge (Gletscherschlucht)
places.near: Short bus (toward Grindelwald Gletscherschlucht) or a pleasant walk from the village centre.
places.order: Entry ticket for the gorge walk; brave the Spiderweb net stretched ~7 m above the rushing water. Berner Oberland Pass gives a discount.
places.notes: Scenic, atmospheric and family-friendly. (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: Walkways and tunnels threaded between 300 m marble-streaked rock walls along the roaring Lütschine, with themed sections on geology, water and myths — and the thrilling 170 m² 'Spiderweb' net suspended over the gorge.
places.goodToKnow: Seasonal — generally open late spring to autumn; closed in winter. Cool and damp inside; light jacket recommended.

places.name: Grosse Scheidegg Pass (Bus & Hikes)
places.near: Yellow GrindelwaldBus from Grindelwald Bahnhof toward Grosse Scheidegg / Schwarzwaldalp; buy tickets at the station booth.
places.order: Take the GrindelwaldBus from Grindelwald station (~36 min) to Grosse Scheidegg, then hike the panoramic path to First (or descend via Waldspitz/Bort).
places.notes: Quieter, photogenic alternative for Wetterhorn views.
places.famousFor: A high saddle beneath the towering Wetterhorn north face, reached on a dramatic postbus ride, and a launch point for tranquil, less-crowded meadow hikes toward First, Bussalp and the Rosenlaui valley.
places.setting: Mountain pass / scenic bus / hiking hub
places.goodToKnow: Summer bus service only (roughly June–October); the road is closed to private traffic. Mountain restaurant at the pass.

places.name: Berggasthaus Waldspitz (Viewpoint & Hut)
places.near: Hike from First past Bachalpsee (~2 h), or take the GrindelwaldBus toward Waldspitz in summer.
places.order: Stop for the terrace views on the descent from Bachalpsee; reachable on foot or by the summer GrindelwaldBus (four times daily).
places.notes: Lesser-known scenic waypoint. (Needs verification of exact 2026 bus times/opening dates)
places.famousFor: A traditional mountain guesthouse with a terrace framing the Eiger, Schreckhorn and Wetterhorn — a scenic waypoint on the First / Bachalpsee circuit and a peaceful photo stop.
places.goodToKnow: Summer season only; check opening before relying on it for a meal or bus connection.

places.name: Big Pintenfritz – World's Longest Sledging Run
places.near: Ascend on foot from Grindelwald-First; the run finishes back near Grindelwald (Oberäll).
places.order: Hike up (~2.5+ hours past Bachalpsee to Faulhorn) with a sledge, then ride the marked run down; rent a sled in the village.
places.notes: Bucket-list winter adventure for the fit. (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: Billed as the longest sledging run in the world / Europe — an epic ~15 km descent (about 1,600 m of drop) from the Faulhorn via Bussalp back to Grindelwald, earned by first hiking up to the summit hut.
places.goodToKnow: Winter/snow season only, conditions-dependent; a serious, high-alpine undertaking — go with good gear and check avalanche/trail status. For an easier fix, Bussalp offers up to ~10.7 km of sledging reached by GrindelwaldBus.

places.name: Bussalp Sledging & Sun Terrace
places.near: Yellow GrindelwaldBus from Grindelwald to the Bussalp sun terrace.
places.order: In winter, take the GrindelwaldBus up and sledge down (rent a sled in the village); in summer, use it as a quiet hiking/viewpoint base.
places.notes: The approachable sledging choice for families.
places.famousFor: A sunny alp with up to ~10.7 km of accessible toboggan runs in winter and a broad panorama terrace — an easy, bus-served sledging option (and a scenic hiking spot in summer).
places.goodToKnow: Sledging is snow-season dependent; bus schedule differs summer vs winter. Family-friendly compared with Big Pintenfritz.

places.name: Schynige Platte Nostalgic Railway & Alpine Garden
places.near: Train to Wilderswil (change at Interlaken Ost), then the Schynige Platte cog railway; ~50 min ascent.
places.order: Return ticket from Wilderswil (~CHF 68 full fare, 2026); walk the Alpine Garden loop and the panoramic ridge, or start the long traverse toward Faulhorn/First.
places.notes: Romantic, retro and superb for flower lovers/couples. (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: An 1893 vintage cogwheel train with wooden-bench carriages climbing to a ridge-top terrace, home to an alpine garden of 600+ native flower species and a front-row Eiger-Mönch-Jungfrau panorama.
places.setting: Historic cog railway / alpine botanical garden
places.goodToKnow: 2026 railway season: 13 June–25 October. Nostalgia trains only run in that window; a beautiful but seasonal day trip from the Grindelwald area.
places.address: Schynige Platte Bahn, Bahnhof, 3812 Wilderswil, Switzerland

places.name: Wengernalpbahn (Grindelwald ↔ Kleine Scheidegg Cog Railway)
places.near: Board at Grindelwald / Grindelwald Terminal (Grund).
places.order: Ride Grindelwald–Kleine Scheidegg (~24 min) for the Eiger-base views; combine with the Männlichen Panorama Trail or continue to Jungfraujoch.
places.notes: A scenic-railway highlight and a key regional connector. (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: The world's longest continuous cog railway, its panorama trains climbing past alpine meadows to Kleine Scheidegg (2,061 m) at the very foot of the Eiger — a scenic ride in its own right and the connector to the Panorama Trail and Jungfraujoch.
places.goodToKnow: Runs year-round with half-hourly panorama trains; the Grindelwald–Kleine Scheidegg line closes for maintenance 26 October–6 November 2026.

places.name: Paragliding Jungfrau – Tandem Flight
places.near: Meet the operator in Grindelwald; take-off is typically reached via a short cable car / gondola ride.
places.order: Panorama tandem flight (often launched after a short gondola ride toward First/Männlichen); book ahead. Prices commonly from ~USD 215–245.
places.notes: Standout splurge experience for couples and thrill-seekers. (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: Soaring on a tandem paraglider parallel to the Eiger North Face, floating above Grindelwald's green valley with the Eiger, Mönch and Jungfrau all around — a bucket-list aerial view.
places.goodToKnow: Highly weather-dependent — flights cancel/reschedule in wind or low cloud; book flexible dates. Confirm exact take-off point and included gondola with the operator.
places.address: Paragliding Jungfrau, Grindelwald-First area, 3818 Grindelwald, Switzerland

places.name: Marmorbruch & Milchbach High Trail (Pfingstegg loop)
places.near: Ride the Pfingstegg cable car up, then follow the marked footpaths.
places.order: From Pfingstegg, walk down via the Marmorbruch (~1 h) to the village; or take the balcony trail toward Milchbach and descend to the Hotel Wetterhorn bus stop.
places.notes: Quiet, history-rich hike away from the crowds. (Needs verification of 2026 restaurant opening dates) (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: A lesser-known descent from Pfingstegg past a historic 18th-century marble quarry (Marmorbruch), and a high balcony trail toward the Milchbach restaurant crossing the striking Breitlouwina rock landscape beneath the glaciers.
places.goodToKnow: Summer hiking-season only; some sections are steep. Restaurants (Marmorbruch, Milchbach) are seasonal — verify opening. Bus connection at Hotel Wetterhorn.

places.name: Trümmelbach Falls (Lauterbrunnen day trip)
places.near: From Grindelwald, train to Zweilütschinen then Lauterbrunnen (~37 min), then Bus 141 (~10 min) to the Trümmelbachfälle stop.
places.order: Entry ticket; ride the internal funicular up, then walk down past all 10 falls. Pair with the free-falling Staubbach Falls in Lauterbrunnen village.
places.notes: Easy, spectacular half-day trip from Grindelwald. (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: Europe's largest subterranean waterfalls — ten glacier-fed falls thundering inside the mountain, reached via a tunnel lift and galleries — in the UNESCO Lauterbrunnen 'Valley of 72 Waterfalls'.
places.goodToKnow: Open daily ~09:00–17:00 April–November (08:30–18:00 in July/August); closed in winter. Cold, wet and loud inside — bring a light jacket.

places.name: C und M
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.goodToKnow: Could not confirm a café named 'C und M'; it falls between Swiss and Bad Hofgastein (Austria) entries in the list — confirm the exact place

places.name: Café 3692
places.near: Short uphill walk from Grindelwald village
places.order: Cappuccino or macchiato; homemade bakes
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Cosy mountain cafe on the sunny Terrassenweg with Wetterhorn views; name refers to the 3692m summit
places.goodToKnow: Run by Myriam and Bruno Kaufmann; charming woodwork interior; limited days (around Fri-Sun)

places.name: EigerBean – The Coffee Roastery
places.cuisine: Third-wave specialty coffee; pastries; coffee beans & gifts
places.near: Dorfstrasse 195, on the main street about a 2-minute walk from the First gondola station and easy from Grindelwald station.
places.order: A pour-over or espresso from the house roast, cold brew with Swiss tonic, the 'Coffee Bon-Bon' (espresso dessert drink), or cascara (coffee-cherry) tea; grab a bag of beans to take home.
places.notes: Best choice for serious coffee lovers; more sleek-modern than cosy-rustic.
places.famousFor: Grindelwald's dedicated specialty-coffee spot, founded 2023 by roaster Marco Münch. Beans roasted in-house (sail-shipped green coffee via Belco, three roast profiles), local Lenk dairy milk, window seats with a mountain outlook.
places.goodToKnow: Small, modern, roastery-style space; typically open daily ~9:00–17:00. Pastries are sourced from a nearby bakery rather than baked on-site.

places.name: QRAFTwerk
places.cuisine: Fresh-baked pastries, cakes, house chocolate, coffee & hot chocolate
places.near: Dorfstrasse 123, central on the main street, easy walk from Grindelwald station and the First/Terminal buses.
places.order: Laminated croissant or chocolate croissant, a cake from the counter, a cappuccino, and the much-praised hot chocolate; pick up a bar of freshly broken chocolate as a gift.
places.notes: Best in-village pick for pastries + hot chocolate and an Instagrammable counter.
places.famousFor: A stylish modern bakery-café opened 2022 with an open production area where you watch the baking and chocolate-making. Highly rated for laminated pastries and hot chocolate (ranked among the top dessert spots in town).
places.goodToKnow: Casual, chilled vibe good for families; some gluten-free options reported; also on Too Good To Go. Eat-in or takeaway.

places.name: Bäckerei-Konditorei-Café Ringgenberg
places.cuisine: Swiss bakery: breads, croissants, konditorei cakes, continental breakfast, coffee
places.near: Dorfstrasse 123 area on the main street, short walk from Grindelwald station and near the First cable-car station.
places.order: Freshly baked buttery Zopf (Swiss plaited bread), a flaky almond croissant, carrot cake, or a konditorei slice with a bean-to-cup coffee; simple breakfast (yogurt, muesli, pastries) works well too.
places.notes: Classic Swiss bakery-café; great value for breakfast and bread.
places.famousFor: A dependable long-running bakery-café right on the main street, known for its wide pastry/bread selection and award-winning bread. A reliable, affordable stop for late breakfast, afternoon tea or a post-walk pause.
places.goodToKnow: Opens early (~7:00). Warm, cosy but often very busy; service can be hit-or-miss per reviews, but product quality and value are consistently praised.

places.name: Bäckerei Wüthrich
places.cuisine: Swiss bakery: pastries, muffins, croissants, apple strudel, sandwiches, hot chocolate
places.near: Ischweg 1, near the Pfingstegg cable-car station; a short walk/bus from the village centre.
places.order: Apple strudel, a chocolate or blueberry muffin, a fresh croissant, and the hot chocolate; grab sandwiches to take up the mountain.
places.notes: A genuine hidden-gem local bakery rather than a scene café. (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: A clean, friendly small-town bakery loved for fresh pastries and 'authentic Swiss hot chocolate.' A local, low-key spot away from the main-street crowds; handy for picnic supplies before a hike.
places.setting: Village bakery with small café seating
places.goodToKnow: Opens ~7:00; mainly takeaway with some indoor and outdoor seating. Staff praised as kind and helpful.

places.name: C und M – Café Bar Restaurant
places.cuisine: Swiss café-restaurant: cakes, apfelstrudel, soups, light meals, coffee & tea
places.near: Almisgässli 1, a short walk from the village junction; if coming from the First cable-car area, the local bus toward the centre passes nearby.
places.order: Homemade Swiss cakes and Apfelstrudel with a coffee or tea; for something savoury, the ravioli or the much-praised lemongrass soup, plus excellent house breads.
places.notes: Great cosy-chalet pick for couples and families wanting cake + a view.
places.famousFor: A charming rustic café in a wooden chalet with big windows framing Eiger views, ideal for brunch or an afternoon tea break. Cosy upstairs sofas and a strong homemade-cake selection; ~4.5 on Tripadvisor and among the town's top-rated spots.
places.goodToKnow: Open daily with extended weekend hours; welcoming, family-friendly service. It does have a bar, but it reads as a cosy café/tea spot, not an alcohol-focused venue.

places.name: Berggasthaus Männlichen (Mountain Brunch)
places.cuisine: Swiss alpine brunch buffet, rösti, pastries, coffee
places.near: Take the V-Cableway gondola from Grindelwald Terminal (~25 min; Terminal is ~5 min by train from Grindelwald station), then a short walk to the summit restaurant.
places.order: The Sunday mountain brunch buffet — Zopf and breads, rösti, bacon and scrambled eggs, muesli, fresh fruit and crêpes; otherwise coffee and cake on the terrace with the panorama.
places.notes: Not a village café — an excursion; go on a clear morning. (Needs verification of current-season dates/prices) (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: The classic 'brunch with a view' experience above Grindelwald: a huge sun terrace and panoramic restaurant facing the Eiger–Mönch–Jungfrau. The Sunday summer Mountain Brunch is a bucket-list family outing.
places.setting: Panoramic mountain restaurant / café with sun terrace
places.goodToKnow: Mountain Brunch runs Sundays roughly June–late October, ~09:00–11:30; a combined cableway + brunch ticket (~CHF 72 adult, less with Half-Fare/GA) is the easy option. Reservations recommended; weather-dependent for the views.

places.name: Cafe LIV
places.area: Mürren (car-free village, Lauterbrunnen Valley — day trip from Grindelwald)
places.cuisine: Coffee, homemade cakes, cookies, toasties, sandwiches
places.near: From Grindelwald: train to Lauterbrunnen, then cable car to Grütschalp + mountain train to Mürren (or via Stechelberg–Schilthornbahn). A scenic half-day outing.
places.order: A carefully prepared coffee with a slice of apple-walnut cake (cream-cheese frosting, caramel) or chocolate cheesecake; the cranberry-and-Brie toastie and vanilla lemonade are signatures.
places.notes: In Mürren, not Grindelwald — included as a nearby Jungfrau-region gem.
places.famousFor: A beloved little café in the heart of car-free Mürren where coffee is made with real care. Handmade upcycled-wood interior (tables built from a single oak tree) and standout home baking; named after the owner's daughter, Olive.
places.goodToKnow: Small and popular; cosy indoor seating. Perfect couples/hidden-gem stop while exploring Mürren.

places.name: InSport Coffee Bar
places.area: Mürren (car-free village — day trip from Grindelwald)
places.cuisine: High-quality espresso drinks with latte art, snacks, Australian pies, smoothies
places.near: Same route as Mürren above (Lauterbrunnen → Grütschalp/Grütschalp train, or Stechelberg cable car). On Höhematte in the village.
places.order: A 'mountain cappuccino' with the Eiger–Mönch–Jungfrau latte art; pair with an Australian-style pie or a smoothie and enjoy the terrace outlook.
places.notes: In Mürren, not Grindelwald — included as a nearby Jungfrau-region gem with the best Eiger-view coffee. (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: A tiny coffee bar tucked inside the InSport shop at ~1,650 m with jaw-dropping views of the Eiger, Mönch and Jungfrau. Known for meticulous coffee — owner insists every cup goes out with latte art (often the three peaks).
places.setting: Specialty coffee bar (inside a sports shop)
places.goodToKnow: Very small; grab-and-go plus a few terrace seats with the big view. Great quick specialty-coffee fix while in Mürren.

places.name: Velo Cafe
places.area: Interlaken (gateway town ~30–40 min by train from Grindelwald)
places.cuisine: Italian-style espresso, brunch, homemade cakes and granola
places.near: Unionsgasse 10, central Interlaken; ~5–10 min walk from Interlaken Ost, and Grindelwald is ~30–40 min by direct train.
places.order: An espresso-based coffee with 'Mum's-recipe' carrot cake; for brunch, the breakfast burrito, the grilled-halloumi breakfast sandwich, or homemade granola.
places.notes: In Interlaken, not Grindelwald — included as a nearby gateway-town brunch/coffee gem.
places.famousFor: A cosy, bike-themed specialty café (vintage bicycles, leather seating, manual Italian espresso machine) that's a favourite brunch and coffee stop in Interlaken — handy on your way in or out of the Grindelwald valley.
places.goodToKnow: Great for a relaxed brunch; fills up on weekends. Fits the couples/family + good-coffee brief; not alcohol-focused.
